summary: Tradeify scored 82/100 when our research team tested and reviewed the popular US futures prop firm. As well as being broker-backed and offering Tradovate, TradingView and NinjaTrader, instant futures funding is also available. The funding options are flexible, and payouts are fast once you're verified. We marked it down for ongoing monthly fees that can add up, email-only support, and basically non-existent education. If you already know what you're doing, it's a clean setup.
pros: Real exchange access (CME, CBOT, COMEX, NYMEX)
cons: Strict payout rules and consistency caps
No weekend trading on any model
High fees for add-ons (Level 2 data, Order Flow+)
Monthly billing continues until manually cancelled
bestFor: Experienced futures traders who want real exchange access, multiple funding paths, and fast payouts — especially intraday scalpers comfortable with Tradovate or TradingView
notIdealFor: Beginner traders who need education and hand-holding, or traders who rely on EAs, copy trading, or weekend holding. Also not ideal if you're in a restricted country

categorySections.challenges: Tradeify runs three futures funding models: Advanced (1-step, intraday trailing drawdown, monthly fees from $69), Growth (1-step, EOD trailing drawdown, monthly fees from $139), and Lightning (instant funding, one-time fees from $349). All models have trailing drawdowns but differ in risk parameters and fee structures. Scored 8/10 for challenges.
categorySections.spreads: Tradeify charges a flat $1.29 per side ($2.58 round turn) across all asset classes. Exchange and regulatory fees vary by contract — Micro Bitcoin can cost $8+ round trip while Micro Ether is around $2.83. Level 1 data is free; Level 2 costs $16–$41/month extra. Scored 7/10 for pricing.
categorySections.markets: Full access to regulated US futures markets through CME, CBOT, COMEX, and NYMEX via Tradovate. Indices, currencies, metals, energies, agriculturals, and crypto futures available. Contract limits based on account size (5–15 standard contracts). EUREX available as paid add-on. Scored 8/10 for markets.
categorySections.platforms: Three platforms supported: Tradovate (web/mobile, default), TradingView (charting-focused, via Tradovate backend), and NinjaTrader (desktop only, advanced automation). All execution routes through Tradovate. Scored 9/10 for platforms.
categorySections.payouts: 90% profit split. Payouts via Rise Pay and Plane Payouts. Requires 10 trading days and 5 profitable days over $200 before first withdrawal. Lightning minimum $1,000 withdrawal. Consistency rules cap daily profit at 35% (Advanced/Growth) or 20% (Lightning). Scored 8/10 for payouts.
categorySections.support: Email-only support during business hours Mon–Fri. Website chatbot redirects to manual support. No phone support, no priority tiers. No formal education, tutorials, or onboarding content.
categorySections.trust: 4.7/5 Trustpilot rating from 1,800+ verified reviews. ~90% five-star reviews. Active Discord community with ~30,000 members. Broker-backed via Tradovate with real exchange access. Extensive restricted countries list. Scored 9/10 for trust.

verdictBlocks.heading: Final Verdict: 82/100
verdictBlocks.body:
Tradeify is a strong pick for futures traders who want real exchange access, multiple funding options, and a clean setup. The challenges are well-defined, with clear risk rules and decent flexibility depending on how you trade. Payouts are quick, and platform access is solid, especially if you're already comfortable with Tradovate or TradingView.

It's less beginner-friendly though. Support can be slow, there's no real education, and once you're funded, there's no room for error. Add-on fees and strict payout rules can trip up newer traders.

faq.q: Is Tradeify a legit futures prop firm?
faq.a: Yes, Tradeify is a legitimate futures prop firm offering real exchange access through CME, CBOT, COMEX, and NYMEX via Tradovate. They've built a strong presence with over 1,800 verified reviews on Trustpilot and a 4.7 out of 5 rating, along with an active Discord community of nearly 30,000 traders. Payouts are processed fast using third-party providers, and rules are clearly outlined on their website.

faq.q: How much is Tradeify per month?
faq.a: Tradeify's monthly costs depend on the challenge type and account size. The Advanced Challenge starts at $69 per month, while the Growth Challenge ranges from $139 to $339. The Lightning Challenge is a one-time payment model starting at $349 with no ongoing fees. Keep in mind, billing continues until you cancel it manually, even if you fail the challenge. Add-ons like Level 2 data or premium tools are charged separately.

Tradeify is a US-based futures prop firm that partners with Tradovate for brokerage and execution. All trading is done through regulated US exchanges, and their backend infrastructure is tied into the CME Group. Their platform access and payout services are aligned with US market hours and compliance rules.

faq.q: What countries are eligible for Tradeify?
faq.a: Most countries can join Tradeify, but some regions are restricted due to broker and compliance rules. You can sign up from the USA, UK, Australia, and most of Europe. However, countries like Afghanistan, North Korea, Russia, and much of Africa and the Middle East are fully blocked. Others, including China, Canada (Ontario), UAE, and South Korea, can access Sim Funded accounts but not Live Funded status.
